attributes.content:
## Overview

The Story (Vertical Video) card gives the workspace a structured home for vertical video/image story format with swipe navigation, captions, and duration.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Story (Vertical Video) usually evolves through drafting, review, and reuse. The card keeps its core content structured enough to be surfaced in different views, linked from related records, and updated by people or automation without losing provenance.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[AI Image]]** -- Story (Vertical Video) can incorporate generated visuals or treat image output as a linked asset.
- **[[Prompt Template]]** -- Prompt-driven drafting or summarization can turn Story (Vertical Video) into a reusable AI-assisted workflow.
- **[[Project]]** -- Story (Vertical Video) can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.
- **[[Task]]** -- Story (Vertical Video) often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Document]]** -- Story (Vertical Video) can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Brand Guide]]** -- Brand Guide can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Story (Vertical Video).

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Story (Vertical Video) as the structured home for vertical video/image story format with swipe navigation, captions, and duration and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Story (Vertical Video) when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Story (Vertical Video) stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
